steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls after "according to some ___" and before "The 2017 data suggested..." Both surrounding pieces are independent clauses, so the blank must supply punctuation that legally ends one independent clause and allows the next to begin.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘economists the’: no punctuation between two independent clauses — a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘economists, the’: a comma alone between two independent clauses is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘economists; as the’: a semicolon must join two independent clauses, but adding "as" makes what follows a dependent clause, which cannot follow a semicolon. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘economists. The’: a period correctly ends the first independent clause; the second begins a new sentence. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
